statements.text: $P(\text{cancer} | +) > $ false positive rate of the test.?
$P(\text{positive test}) < P(\text{cancer})$.?
$P(\neg\text{cancer} | -) < P(\neg\text{cancer})$.?
$P(\text{positive test}) < P(\text{cancer}|+)$.?
$P(\text{cancer}, +) < P(\text{cancer}|+)$.?
For all joint distributions: $\mathbb{E}[X+Y] = \mathbb{E}[X] + \mathbb{E}[Y]$.?
For all joint distributions: $\text{Var}[X+Y] = \text{Var}[X] + \text{Var}[Y]$.?
For all joint distributions: $\mathbb{E}[X] = \mathbb{E}_Y[\mathbb{E}_X[X|Y]]$.?
For all joint distributions: $\text{Var}[X] = \mathbb{E}_Y[\text{Var}_X[X|Y]] + \text{Var}_Y[\mathbb{E}_X[X|Y]]$.?
For all joint distributions: $\mathbb{E}[X \times Y] = \mathbb{E}[X] \times \mathbb{E}[Y]$.?

context:

N: 70 correct, 20 as B, 10 as M (total 100).

Column totals: N=80, B=90, M=30.

statements.text: Recall for class M is $0.50$ (50%).?
Precision for class B is higher than its Recall.?
F1-score for class N is exactly $0.75$.?
False alarm rate (predict M, actual N) > miss rate (predict N or B, actual M).?
Precision for class N is higher than Precision for class B.?
The MLE for $\mu$ is the sample mean $\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n x_i$.?
The MLE for variance is an unbiased estimator of the true population variance.?
If the true mean $\mu$ were known, the MLE for variance would be $\frac{1}{n-1}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i-\mu)^2$.?
The log-likelihood function is a linear function of $\sigma^2$.?
The maximum of the likelihood $L$ is achieved at the same $(\mu, \sigma^2)$ as the maximum of $\log L$.?

context:
Parent: 50 positive, 50 negative (100 total).
$v_1$: 30 samples (20+, 10−). $v_2$: 30 samples (10+, 20−). $v_3$: 40 samples (20+, 20−).

statements.text: The Gini index of the parent node is exactly $0.5$.?
$\text{Gini}(v_3) > \text{Gini}(v_1)$.?
$\text{Gini}(v_1) = \text{Gini}(v_2)$.?
The Information Gain for this split is greater than $0.15$ bits.?
The reduction in Gini index from parent to children is greater than $0.04$.?

context:
60 bought (B=1): 42 tech (C=T), 48 discount (D=1).
40 did not buy (B=0): 12 tech, 8 discount.

statements.text: The prior probability $P(B=1) = 0.6$.?
The Naïve Bayes assumption implies $P(C, D) = P(C) \cdot P(D)$ (marginal independence).?
For $C=F, D=0$, Naïve Bayes predicts $B=1$ (will buy).?
The ratio $P(B=1|C=T,D=1) / P(B=0|C=T,D=1) = 14$.?
As max_depth $\to \infty$, training error decreases but variance typically increases.?
A decision tree with max_depth = 1 has higher variance and lower bias than deeper trees.?
Increasing the minimum number of samples required to split a node increases model bias.?
The bias component of error represents the sensitivity of predictions to small fluctuations in the training set.?
If a decision tree perfectly classifies every noisy training example, the test error is dominated by variance and irreducible error.?

context: Model: $\texttt{nn.Linear(5, 1)} — 5$ weights + 1 bias. Training loop: 20 epochs over $\texttt{train\_dl}$.

statements.text: The model contains exactly 6 learnable parameters.?
The code is certain to perform exactly 20 gradient updates.?
The code works for all classification problems regardless of the number of class labels.?
\texttt{loss.backward()} computes gradients for weights but not for the bias.?
The optimizer uses the stochastic version of gradient descent.?

context: Code implements a Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA)-like classifier with per-class mean and covariance.

statements.text: The implementation calculates a class-specific covariance matrix for each class.?
$\texttt{log\_gaussian}$ will work correctly when $D=10$ but a class has only 5 training samples.?
$\texttt{log\_gaussian}$ does not return the mathematically exact log-density of a multivariate Gaussian.?
The implementation uses gradient descent for parameter optimisation.?
If $\texttt{learn()}$ used a shared covariance for all classes, the decision boundaries would be non-linear.?
$R_{\text{emp}}(h)$ is an unbiased estimator of $R(h)$ for a fixed $h$, but is biased for the ERM hypothesis $\hat{h}$.?
The generalisation gap is guaranteed to decrease at a linear rate as $n$ increases.?
If $\mathcal{H} \subseteq \mathcal{H}'$, then $R_{\text{emp}}(\hat{h}') \leq R_{\text{emp}}(\hat{h})$.?
To ensure $R(\hat{h}) \leq R_{\text{emp}}(\hat{h}) + \epsilon$ with confidence $1-\delta$, $n$ must scale linearly with $|\mathcal{H}|$.?
The bound $R(\hat{h}) \leq R_{\text{emp}}(\hat{h}) + \sqrt{\frac{\ln|\mathcal{H}|+\ln(1/\delta)}{2n}}$ holds for all finite $\mathcal{H}$ but not necessarily all distributions.?
If algorithm A has smaller generalisation error than B on one dataset, A is expected to outperform B across all distributions.?
For any learning algorithm, there exists at least one distribution where its error cannot be reduced by enlarging the training set.?
NFL implies that inductive bias is necessary for better-than-random generalisation on any specific task.?
The NFL theorem applies only to parametric predictors, not non-parametric models like k-NN.?
NFL implies that for any distribution with a perfect classifier, any algorithm converges to zero generalisation error if $m \geq |\mathcal{X}|/8$.?
A concept is PAC learnable if a polynomial-in-$1/\epsilon$ sample size suffices to achieve error $< \epsilon$ with probability $\geq 1-\delta$, for fixed $\delta$.?
A larger hypothesis space $\mathcal{H}$ requires fewer training samples for the same error level.?
Sample complexity for a finite $\mathcal{H}$ grows logarithmically in $|\mathcal{H}|$.?
The confidence parameter $\delta$ is the maximum rate of incorrect label predictions at generalisation time.?
The Fundamental Theorem of Statistical Learning states that $\mathcal{H}$ is PAC learnable iff its VC dimension is finite.?

section: Question 13: VC Dimension of Axis-Aligned Boxes in $\mathbb{R}^3$
context: For axis-aligned boxes in $\mathbb{R}^d$, the VC dimension is $2d$. In $\mathbb{R}^3$: $\text{VC}(\mathcal{H}) = 6$.

statements.text: There exists a set of 6 points in $\mathbb{R}^3$ that can be shattered by $\mathcal{H}$.?
$\text{VC}(\mathcal{H}) = 4$ in $\mathbb{R}^3$.?
There exists a set of 4 points in $\mathbb{R}^3$ for which every labelling is achievable by some box.?
For $n > 10$, the growth function satisfies $\Pi_\mathcal{H}(n) < 2^n$.?
There does not exist a set of 5 points in $\mathbb{R}^3$ that can be shattered by $\mathcal{H}$.?
